# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd default authorization policy for a namespace. Configure policy to handle services without explicit authorization rules. Set up default behavior for service-to-service communication. This is for our service mesh policy framework.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent linkerd policy default allow.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: linkerd_policy_default_allow

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    config.linkerd.io/default-inbound-policy: "deny"
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta3
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: default-server
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Default server for services without explicit authorization"
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels: {}
  port: http
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Default deny policy - requires explicit authorization"
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: Server
    name: default-server
  requiredAuthenticationRefs: []
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
metadata:
  name: mesh-authenticated
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Require mTLS authentication from mesh identities"
spec:
  identities:
    - "*.secure-namespace.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: authenticated-mesh-only
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Allow only authenticated mesh traffic with explicit service accounts"
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: Server
    name: default-server
  requiredAuthenticationRefs:
    - name: mesh-authenticated
      kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
      group: policy.linkerd.io
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: NetworkAuthentication
metadata:
  name: cluster-internal-only
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Restrict to cluster internal networks only"
spec:
  networks:
    - cidr: 10.0.0.0/8
    - cidr: 172.16.0.0/12
    - cidr: 192.168.0.0/16
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: network-restricted
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Additional network-level restrictions"
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: Server
    name: default-server
  requiredAuthenticationRefs:
    - name: cluster-internal-only
      kind: NetworkAuthentication
      group: policy.linkerd.io
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta3
kind: HTTPRoute
metadata:
  name: default-route
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Default HTTP route with security headers"
spec:
  parentRefs:
    - name: default-server
      kind: Server
      group: policy.linkerd.io
  rules:
    - matches:
        - path:
            type: PathPrefix
            value: "/"
      filters:
        - type: RequestHeaderModifier
          requestHeaderModifier:
            add:
              - name: X-Mesh-Authenticated
                value: "true"
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
metadata:
  name: specific-service-accounts
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Explicit allowlist of authorized service accounts"
spec:
  identityRefs:
    - kind: ServiceAccount
      name: authorized-service-1
      namespace: secure-namespace
    - kind: ServiceAccount
      name: authorized-service-2
      namespace: secure-namespace
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: explicit-service-account-policy
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Least privilege - only specific service accounts allowed"
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: Server
    name: default-server
  requiredAuthenticationRefs:
    - name: specific-service-accounts
      kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
      group: policy.linkerd.io
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: authorized-service-1
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Authorized service account with mesh identity"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: authorized-service-2
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Authorized service account with mesh identity"
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta3
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: metrics-server
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Separate server for metrics endpoints"
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels: {}
  port: metrics
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: metrics-prometheus-only
  namespace: secure-namespace
  annotations:
    description: "Restrict metrics access to Prometheus service account"
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: Server
    name: metrics-server
  requiredAuthenticationRefs:
    - name: prometheus-authentication
      kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
      group: policy.linkerd.io
---
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1alpha1
kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
metadata:
  name: prometheus-authentication
  namespace: secure-namespace
spec:
  identities:
    - "prometheus.linkerd-viz.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"
